mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and driving immediate equity price appreciation.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer credit (RSAFS) and persistent core inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals unsustainable demand overheating, prompting aggressive policy tightening that compresses equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 52 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) during a period of constrained housing supply (HOUST below 600k) indicates manufacturing-led growth that outpaces real estate drag, supporting broad market rallies over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns average below zero when INDPRO expands while HOUST remains under 600.0, contradicting the growth premium premise.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) directly validates corporate revenue trajectories, generating positive momentum in equity prices over the following week as analysts upgrade earnings estimates.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between GDPC1 quarterly growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, borrowing costs suppress capital expenditure and consumer durable demand, systematically depressing forward earnings and equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of observations where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5 percent for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, driving sustained equity outperformance over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative directional information coefficient over any 3-year rolling window, indicating decoupling from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp spike in consumer credit (RSAFS) coinciding with a housing market contraction (HOUST falling > 5 percent month-over-month) triggers a flight-to-quality rotation out of cyclical equities, depressing short-term market levels.
failure_condition: Equity markets produce positive 5-day returns in over 50 percent of triggered episodes, disproving the flight-to-quality mechanism.
